intro.title: Hello, I'm Canning Li
intro.desc:
Cultural & Creative Product Development · Brand Strategy
Stationed at Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum, leading venue IP branding and creative product development
B.S. Tourism Management, Southeast University / M.S. Sustainable Tourism, CUHK

recent.title: Recent Work · First Two Months at the Museum

item1.title: Supplier System & Procurement Management
item1.desc: Connected with 10+ supplier categories, managed the full chain from evaluation → negotiation → contracting, and established a tiered supplier management system.

item2.title: Kunpeng IP Brand Strategy
item2.desc: Led the Kunpeng IP brand strategy: mapped cross-team priorities and timelines, produced brand playbook and product development roadmap, deployed to GitHub Pages for team collaboration.

item3.title: Social Media Optimization
item3.desc: Took over the museum's Douyin and RED (Xiaohongshu) accounts, optimized content strategy and editorial direction, improved engagement and established daily operational workflows.

item1.desc: Special Prize in the National Red Tourism Creative Planning Competition. A brand strategy integrating red culture with green and digital marketing.

item2.title: Wellness Tourism Research
item2.desc: National innovation project: post-pandemic wellness tourism study based on Cognitive Appraisal Theory. Published in Modern Tourism and presented at an academic conference.

project1.title: Red Tourism Creative Product
project1.desc:
Special Prize, 11th National Red Tourism Creative Planning Competition.

Highlights:
1) Mined Jiaxing's natural and cultural resources, extracting red and heritage cultural elements;
2) Analyzed red tourism market trends and designed interactive DIY creative products that blend culture, practicality, and sustainability;
3) Integrated red tourism with green marketing, digital marketing, and brand-building strategies.

Awarded Special Prize for a complete brand strategy and product design.

project2.title: Wellness Tourism Research
project2.desc:
National Undergraduate Innovation & Entrepreneurship Program: Post-pandemic wellness tourism development based on Cognitive Appraisal Theory.

Research:
1) Collected 400+ survey responses using quantitative methods based on Cognitive Appraisal Theory;
2) Built a Structural Equation Model (SEM) of tourist cognition, emotion, and behavioral intention;
3) Identified key decision-making factors and proposed development recommendations.

Published in Modern Tourism and presented at the 2021 China Tourism Research Annual Conference.

project3.title: Kunpeng IP Brand Strategy
project3.desc:
Shenzhen Science & Technology Museum brand IP initiative.

Scope:
1) Mapped cross-team priorities, timelines, and collaboration workflows into a full brand execution plan;
2) Built a card-based priority framework for task visualization and progress tracking;
3) Developed and deployed a web platform via GitHub Pages for online team collaboration.

Delivered the IP strategy from concept to execution through structured project management.
